title: Link-Bearing Elements, Relations, Base URLs, and Crawl Graph · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in semantic provenance-rich link graph and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, embedded, form, media, and alternate URL-bearing HTML elements.
Use first valid base semantics against final response URL and retain accepted and rejected base evidence.
Interpret rel tokens, canonical, alternate, hreflang, download, media, nofollow, and target under explicit owner policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Link Elements
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 4138 during an owner-approved community information audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify link elements, base and relation semantics, rejected references, and provenance-rich graph edges; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, embedded, form, media, and alternate URL-bearing HTML elements.

Inspect the Link Elements trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Link Elements.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, embedded, form, media, and alternate url-bearing html elements. For Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Link Elements, keep Link Elements connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish navigation, resource, metadata, embedded, form, media, and alternate URL-bearing HTML elements. Artifact under review: semantic provenance-rich link graph.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Link Elements, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Link Elements record says semantic provenance-rich link graph looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Link Elements.

For inspect the failure record, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Link Elements for semantic provenance-rich link graph.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Edge Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-edge-evidence-42g0yf" that uses case 8379 to classify link elements, base and relation semantics, rejected references, and provenance-rich graph edges. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Edge Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Edge Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Edge Evidence, keep Edge Evidence connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
